iteration1.changes: Baseline — original skill with D3 DOM API patterns (append/join)

iteration2.changes: Rewrote skill to use template literal strings for SVG markup with innerHTML injection instead of D3 DOM API. Fixed has-svg and has-path for both tasks, but bar-count still failed because .map() produces only one <rect in the template literal source

iteration3.changes: Switched to two-step generation: write a Node.js generator script that pre-computes all positions and writes chart.html with fully expanded static SVG (each <rect>, <path>, <text> as literal markup with numeric coordinates). This ensures all SVG elements appear as literal tags in the HTML source.

rootCause: Tests validate chart.html by checking for literal SVG element strings (<svg, <rect, <path) in the file source. D3's DOM API (.append/.join) creates elements at runtime via JavaScript, so they never appear in the static HTML. The fix: generate static HTML with pre-rendered SVG using a two-step build process.
